commands.review_summary:
The PR bounds the Tlon Urbit SSE subscribe non-OK error-body read at 8 KiB and adds helper-level bounded-read tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +94. Total +103 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible, not locally reproduced: current main reads a non-OK subscribe PUT response with unbounded `response.text()`, but this read-only review did not run a live oversized-body OOM probe.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed because the real behavior proof gate requires production-path evidence; no ClawSweeper repair job should replace that proof on an external PR.

target.title: fix(tlon): bound Urbit subscribe errorText read at 8 KiB to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`extensions/tlon/src/urbit/sse-client.ts:156` calls `await response.text().catch(() => "")` to read the diagnostic error body when the Urbit SSE subscribe response is non-OK and non-204. A hostile or misbehaving ship could otherwise stream an unbounded body into memory, OOMing the channel worker.

## Why This Change Was Made

The plugin SDK provides `readResponseWithLimit` in `openclaw/plugin-sdk/response-limit-runtime` for exactly this pattern. We previously used it for tlon's auth response (#97730, open); this PR closes the remaining tlon surface — the SSE subscribe path.

The fix mirrors #97730's shape:
- 1 import: `readResponseWithLimit` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/response-limit-runtime`
- 1 inline constant: `MAX_TLON_SUBSCRIBE_ERROR_BODY_BYTES = 8 * 1024` (Urbit errors are short — "not authenticated", "no subscription", etc.)
- 1 line replacement: `await readResponseWithLimit(...).then(b => b.toString("utf8")).catch(() => "")`

8 KiB matches the per-call byte budget of tlon's other bounded reads and is enough for Urbit's typical short error strings while capping worst-case OOM.

## Evidence

Boundary probe on this PR branch — input is `not authenticated` (17 bytes) plus 32 KiB hostile body, both against an 8 KiB cap:

```
[32 KiB hostile body, cap=8 KiB]
  throws: Content too large: 32768 bytes (limit: 8192 bytes)   ← cap enforced, stream cancelled
                                                               ← sse-client .catch(() => "") absorbs the throw

[17-byte error, cap=8 KiB]
  utf8: "not authenticated"   ← verbatim pass-through for normal errors
```

The `.catch(() => "")` chain in the source mirrors the production behavior: hostile bodies are dropped (worker does not OOM), short errors pass through unchanged.

## After-fix Proof

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/tlon/src/urbit/sse-client.errortext.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

```
 ✓ |extension-messaging| extensions/tlon/src/urbit/sse-client.errortext.test.ts > tlon urbit sse-client errorText bounded read > readResponseWithLimit throws when a hostile ship body exceeds the cap 142ms
 ✓ |extension-messaging| extensions/tlon/src/urbit/sse-client.errortext.test.ts > tlon urbit sse-client errorText bounded read > readResponseWithLimit passes through a short Urbit error verbatim 7ms
 ✓ |extension-messaging| extensions/tlon/src/urbit/sse-client.errortext.test.ts > tlon urbit sse-client errorText bounded read > sse-client.ts catch fallback swallows the overflow and yields empty errorText 6ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 11.62s
```

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json extensions/tlon/src/urbit/sse-client.ts extensions/tlon/src/urbit/sse-client.errortext.test.ts` → EXIT=0
- `git diff --check openclaw/main...HEAD` → clean

## Diff scope

```
 extensions/tlon/src/urbit/sse-client.ts                  |  11 +++++++++++-
 extensions/tlon/src/urbit/sse-client.errortext.test.ts   | 96 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++ (new)
 2 files changed, 104 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
```

## Security & Privacy

No user-facing behavior change for normal (short) Urbit error bodies — they pass through verbatim. For hostile bodies, the read caps at 8 KiB and the SSE worker cancels the upstream stream instead of buffering the full body.

## Compatibility

Plugin SDK export (`openclaw/plugin-sdk/response-limit-runtime`) is already in `package.json` exports for `@openclaw/plugin-sdk`. The helper is dependency-free per `packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.ts`. No new dependency added at the package level.

## What was not tested

Live Urbit ship connection with a real hostile 4xx/5xx body. The fix is a one-line bounded-read guard; the helper is covered by unit tests in `packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.ts` and our previous bounded-read PRs (#97730 tlon auth, #97721 discord gateway, #97876 openai-completions) all used the same inline-loopback proof pattern. Integration coverage at this layer is intentionally out of scope.

## Related

- Our #97730: same fix shape on tlon auth.ts — uses the same helper and same `.catch(() => "")` pattern.
- Our #97721: same fix shape on discord gateway-metadata.ts.
- Plugin SDK source: `packages/media-core/src/read-response-with-limit.ts` (re-exported via `openclaw/plugin-sdk/response-limit-runtime`).
